verbGlossary.LOGGED AT: {source} is formally recorded and entered into the system at this lifecycle {target}, creating the initial tracking record.
verbGlossary.TRACKED THROUGH: {source}is actively monitored and updated as it moves through this in-progress {target} during the project.
verbGlossary.RETIRED AT: {source}is formally closed out and archived when it reaches this terminal {target}, no longer requiring active monitoring.
verbGlossary.ORIGINATES IN: {source} is first created in this editable draft {target} before formal review begins.
verbGlossary.BASELINED UPON: {source} is locked as the official project baseline when {target} is reached.
verbGlossary.ASSESSED DURING: {source} or assessment is actively evaluated and scored while held at this review {target}.
verbGlossary.ACTIVATES AT: The risk {target} fires and the associated risk becomes active when conditions move it to this open status.
verbGlossary.ESCALATES VIA: {source}is automatically elevated to senior project leadership through this escalation {target} when thresholds are breached.
verbGlossary.EVALUATED DURING: {source}is performed and scored while {target} is held at this review status.
verbGlossary.LOCKED UPON: {source} becomes read-only and official once {target} is reached, preventing further adjustment.
verbGlossary.AWAITS: {source} is queued and waiting for action at {target}; no execution until someone acts.
verbGlossary.AUTHORIZED UPON: {source} is formally approved for execution when {target} is reached.
verbGlossary.INITIATED AT: {source} is first opened and assigned at {target}, beginning the remediation effort.
verbGlossary.EXECUTED THROUGH: {source} is actively being carried out on the jobsite while in this in-progress {target}.
verbGlossary.VERIFIED AT: {source} is confirmed complete and effective when it reaches this closed {target} after field verification.
verbGlossary.ESCALATES TO: {source} automatically transitions to {target} when its deadline or threshold is exceeded.
verbGlossary.BOUND BY: {source} is constrained by this due date, establishing a contractual or schedule deadline for completion.
verbGlossary.RELEASED UPON: {source} funds are disbursed from the reserve when {target} is achieved by the draw request.
verbGlossary.ROUTES THROUGH: {source} passes through {target} chain or workflow pattern as part of its lifecycle processing.
verbGlossary.RESERVED DURING: {source} funds are set aside and allocated to the project budget during this financial planning stage.
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTED AT: {source}is formally recorded when the response reaches this closed {target}.
verbGlossary.FLAGGED AT: {source}is identified and marked when a field inspection produces this deficient {target}.
verbGlossary.DISPOSITIONED VIA: {source}is evaluated and given a formal pass/fail determination through this inspection {target} pattern.
verbGlossary.SUBMITTED AT: {source}requirement documentation is formally submitted for review at {target}.
verbGlossary.CERTIFIED UPON: {source}requirement is verified as meeting contractual coverage thresholds when {target} is granted.
verbGlossary.CERTIFIES VIA: The surety bond obtains formal authentication and binding execution through {target}.
verbGlossary.COMPUTED AT: {source} is calculated at this specific {target} or financial stage as data becomes available.
verbGlossary.TRACKED BY: {source} is monitored over time using {target} performance indicator.
